input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
Here's how to make your MAUI page theme-aware:

## XAML — `AppThemeBinding`

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=White, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="15">

        <Label Text="Welcome!"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=Black, Dark=White}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#333333, Dark=#CCCCCC}" />

        <Button Text="Click Me"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=White, Dark=Black}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The `{AppThemeBinding Light=..., Dark=...}` markup extension automatically reacts to `Application.RequestedTheme` changes — no event wiring needed.

## C# Alternative — `SetAppTheme`

Every `BindableObject` has `SetAppThemeColor` (for colors) and the general `SetAppTheme<T>`:

```csharp
// Colors
myLabel.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty, Colors.Black, Colors.White);
this.SetAppThemeColor(ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
                      Colors.White, Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E"));

// Non-color properties (e.g., image sources)
myImage.SetAppTheme<string>(Image.SourceProperty,
                            "sun.png", "moon.png");
```

Arguments are `(BindableProperty, lightValue, darkValue)`.

## Tips

- **Resource dictionaries** scale better — define colors once in `App.xaml` and reference them:
  ```xml
  <!-- App.xaml -->
  <Color x:Key="PageBg">White</Color>  <!-- in AppThemeBinding -->
  ```
- Both approaches respond **live** when the user toggles system dark mode; no restart required.
- To force a theme, set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark;`.
